Record.statement_ur: الگورتھم کی تعریف کریں اور ایک مسئلے کو حل کرنے میں اس کے کردار کی وضاحت کریں۔
Record.answer_en: An algorithm is a set of instructions that are developed to solve a problem, i.e. an algorithm is a finite set of steps that, if followed, will lead to the completion of a particular task. An algorithm is a collection of steps to solve a problem called natural language. The simplest form of algorithm is the stepwise algorithm. It consists of a series of steps. That is, an algorithm is a complete procedure for solving a problem that expresses the logic of the program in written form. Algorithms should be clear, definitive and effective. For example, to solve the problem of making tea, the following steps must be taken: Start, take a kettle, pour water into it, put the kettle on the fire, add sugar and milk, wait for it to boil, turn the kettle on. Remove from fire, end.

Record.statement_en: Suppose a problem has multiple algorithms. How would you choose the most efficient one? Explain with examples.
Record.statement_ur: اگر ایک مسئلے کے ایک سے زیادہ کئی حل ہوتو آپ ان میں سےبہترین حل کا انتخاب کیسے کریں گے؟ مثالوں کے ساتھ استدلال کریں۔
Record.answer_en: There may be more than one algorithm to solve a problem, which one is better depends on the performance of the algorithm. The performance of any algorithm can be evaluated on two grounds. Number of Steps: An algorithm is considered more efficient if it shows the correct result in fewer steps. Computer Memory Usage: Algorithms store the information they use in the computer's memory. An algorithm that consumes less space or memory is considered a better algorithm than an algorithm that consumes more memory. It is also possible that one algorithm solves a problem using less memory and in more steps while another algorithm solves the same problem in fewer steps but using more memory. depends on the relevance of the condition. Example: We take two algorithms that solve the same problem, one algorithm solves the problem in N steps while the other solves the same problem in N2 steps and the first algorithm is considered better.

Record.statement_en: Explain types of test data.
Record.statement_ur: ٹیسٹ ڈیٹا کی اقسام بیان کریں۔
Record.answer_en: Data whose results are already known is called test data. Complete and parallel test data is very important to improve the quality of any solution. Each phase requires different types of test data. The following are some types of test data: Exact test data: This test data consists of input that exactly matches the requirements of an algorithm.If an algorithm takes values â€‹â€‹from 10 to 1000, then Only values â€‹â€‹up to 100 will be valid in the test data. Rare test: This test data does not conform to the requirements of the algorithm. It is also important to see how the algorithm reacts to incorrect input and prompts the user for correct input. Boundary Test Data: In this test, a solution is tested for extreme values. For example we can input a value of 0 or a large value to find the interest. Mis-sampling of data: It is very wise to test an algorithm for mis-sampling eg where number input is required. English alphabet should be given. Unavailable data: It is also a necessary practice to test the algorithm by giving it less input than it needs, for example if an algorithm asks the user for his driver's license number. And it is not possible to input data. This is important to know in order to see how the algorithm should behave in such a situation.
